desc:
Gnarled, twisted trees grow wildly with giant crowns that blot out the sky
across rolling hills. Scraggly vines wrap themselves around the great trunks as
they squeeze and suck the life from the towering woods. The uneven ground is
covered with dead leaves as well as small shrubs as the hills continue rising
steadily toward the west.
name: Forested Hill

desc:
A great crown of ghastly ash and fir trees sit atop a large hill in the forest.
The trees' roots are strangely thin yet long, snaking along the ground in an
irregular fashion. Numerous man-sized boulders lay scattered along the hill,
creating deep crevices between root and stone. The uneven ground decreases in
elevation to the south.
name: Large Wooded Foothill

desc:
A forested valley is populated by sinister dark trees that seem to leer in every
direction above the ground. The air feels stifled and ominous, as if the forest
is silently filled with a brewing wrath. Large green mushrooms as wide as tree
trunks grow here and exude a sickly, deathly smell.
name: Dip in the Forest

desc:
Ashen trees with blackened leaves stretch menacingly up out of sight into the
darkness of the lower canopy. Treacherous uneven slabs of hard grey stone cover
the ground with many interweaving vines growing along the tops of the slabs.
Stiff sharp bushes fill every crack in the stone, scratching against trunks and
roots as they survive in fissures too small for the mammoth trees themselves to
occupy.
name: Forested Mountains

desc:
An isolated hilltop, surrounded by steep slopes and deep crevices, is crowned by
a dense grove of ancient trees. Their twisted, moss-covered trunks disappear
into a dark green canopy so impenetrable that no hint of the sky beyond can be
seen. The forest floor is covered in a thick layer of fallen leaves that serves
as a home for fungi as well as various types of mushrooms that thrive among the
decaying vegetation.
name: Isolated Hilltop

desc:
Twisted oaks and firs grow ravenously in all directions as their outstretched
branches seem to grasp for any signs of life. The darkened forest floor has no
undergrowth and shows no signs of life other than grey and black spotted
mushrooms that only grow in the shadows of great round boulders. The steep rocky
terrain harbours hidden valleys and sudden escarpments camoflauged by the dark
canopy and long leaf-covered branches.
name: Twisted Tree-covered Mountain

desc:
The forest floor is covered by long flat slabs of hulking grey stone as it rises
steadily on the steep mountainside. Blackened pine trees and dark oaks grow in
the gaps between stones, their hard gnarled trunks cracking and splitting the
rock ever more as time passes in the darkness below. Both branch and bough
extend to create an impenetrable forest canopy resulting in stuffy dusty air
with only the faintest hint of dark green light penetrating the canopy during
the day.
name: Dark Wooded Mountainside

desc:
Gnarled oak trees lean ominously over rocky scrambles that dominate the
mountainous landscape amid the forest. Large boulders cover the ground while
tangly vines weave their way along the tree trunks and around the great stones.
The rocky ground descends to the east even as the elevation increases to the
west.
name: Boulder-strewn Forest

desc:
Tall oak and fir trees reach to the unseen sky, blocking any possible light from
reaching the ground. Only a few small withered vines and scraggly bushes manage
to survive in such conditions on the ground itself amidst the choking atmosphere
of the massive dominating trees.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
A deep chasm with steep, slippery walls cuts this part of the Emyn-nu-Fuin off
from the mountains to the south and east. Ancient, gnarled trees grow up to the
very edge of the chasm, their trunks twisting this way and that as if attempting
to escape the strangling vines that ruthlessly encircle them. The canopy above
is dense enough to block out the sky. The light that finds its way along the
forest floor from the gap created by the chasm will never be enough to dispel
the gloom that lingers here.
name: Chasm Edge

desc:
The mountains of the Emyn-nu-Fuin climb toward the north-east as they slowly
transform into taller fir-clad peaks. The terrain is very rugged as deep chasms
and endless ravines clash with sudden cliff-walls rising up into the darkness of
the forest above. Amidst this inhospitable terrain, crooked trees and sprawling
bushes flourish amidst the cracks and crevices of the cold stone.
name: Jagged Crags of the Emyn-nu-Fuin

desc:
The tree-covered hills roll in all directions, gradually descending in height to
the south and east. Dense thickets fill in the gaps between clumps of dark,
gloomy trees. Intermixed with occasional patches of boulders protruding from the
earth are the clenching roots of black oaks and dark pines. A steep slope leads
downward to a dark valley.
name: Hilly Forests of the Emyn-nu-Fuin

desc:
The hilly land seems to be locked in a preternatural gloom as drooping trees
whisper soft rumours to each other with each gust of wind. Animal noises are
muted and the air itself feels tense and oppressive. Skeletal roots erupt from
the rocky terrain like bony claws intent on making travel difficult and
tiresome.
name: Gloomy Hills

desc:
Along this forested ridge, the trees grow tall and in abundance, their branches
hanging low and intertwined with neighbouring trees, creating a dense canopy of
limbs and leaves. Grey and green lichen is draped across the branches, dangling
low and touching the various shrubs and thorny bushes that sprout up next to the
trees.
name: Shaded Bluff

desc:
The terrain rises up from the surrounding hills to this overgrown ridge. The
trees growing here are short and squat, and covered in moss and vines weighing
their branches down so that they almost rest on the ground. Rubble and boulders
form a steep slope up to a higher plateau that rises above the forest to the
east.
name: Forested Butte

desc:
Large, sprawling shrubs and bushes flourish on the slopes and on top of a green
knoll. A few tall oaks grow on the slopes, spreading their branches over the
undergrowth. The undergrowth is dominated by barberry bushes with flowers and
berries in shades of yellow, red and white.
name: Barberry Hilltop

desc:
The jumbled woods of this area feature short and gnarled oak trees, twisting
their branches in an upward angle. Black briar bushes with spindly arms grow in
clusters around most trees, causing a rasping, scratching noise as they scrape
against the trunks. Dead leaves and detritus are scattered across the forest
floor, creating a foul yet earthy odour.
name: Prickling Grove

desc:
Short and squat thorny bushes fully dominate the undergrowth, creating a nearly
impenetrable barrier to traverse through. The spindly branches slowly move in
the stale, humid air of these woods. A few solemn oak trees stand tall among the
briars. Their creaking, rustling branches seem to tear and rend at the air. The
wood is alive with noises and the trees' crowns flutter with dark moths and
black butterflies.
name: Dreary Briars

desc:
The barren nature of this forest creates an eerie atmosphere. A noxious, gaseous
cloud rises up from the muddy surface covering the forest floor, as if terrible
things rotted beneath the ground and released these toxic fumes. A few pale
trees can be seen rising up through the mist, with their gnarled roots hidden
under the wisps of gas.
name: Desolate Woods

desc:
The densely forested terrain of the surrounding Mirkwood finds no foot-holds on
this plateau. The ground is bare or consists of moss-covered rock, with boulders
and jutting crags scattered across its surface. Being elevated above the forest,
the view from this peak is quite breath-taking. Resilient grass and hardy weeds
still manage to find cracks and crevices in which to take root on this lofty,
stony apex.
name: Lithic Panorama

desc:
Dense, dark vegetation grows thick through these woods, the blades of bushes and
trees in shades of green so dark it often appears black. Black oak trees sprawl
across the landscape, their roots crawling in and out of a soil covered in green
moss. A patch of nightshade bushes, surrounded by spotted mushrooms, grows at
the centre of a grove of towering oaks.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
The trees in this forest grow short and squat, with stunted branches growing at
odd angles. Black lichen hangs thickly from the twisted limbs, swinging gently
whenever a breeze rustles the leaves above. Vines wrap around the short trees,
oozing a stinking liquid out of their wooden pores.
name: Bent Woods

desc:
The menacing nature of these woods is derived from its impenetrable nature; the
trees grow closely together, with vegetation of all sorts growing between them.
Branches droop low, with the vines draping them reaching the ground. The dense
growth literally blocks any light coming from the outside.
name: Overgrown Woodland

desc:
The forest is thin here on a hill rising above the surrounding Mirkwood. Dark
blue-green crab grass grows tall and dense across this knoll, with a few bushes
sprouting up in the recesses of the slope where most shade can be found. The
occasional fir can be seen, few and far in between; the rich smell of grass
permeates the air.
name: Grassy Knoll

desc:
Rows of massive trees dominate this wooded region. Their branches growing long
and thick, the leaves wide and flat. Ample shade and fertile soil has provided a
home to a myriad of plants and fungi. Spiny shrubs grow tall and spotted
mushrooms are found in abundance around the gnarled tree roots.
name: Ancient Oaks

desc:
The terrain rises up out of the forest, affording a better view of the
surroundings. The dark green vegetation that thrives here sprouts up from the
rich soil. An earthen smell is potent throughout this area, the moisture
evaporating off of the ground. Tall trees grow with twisting limbs, arms covered
with grey lichen.
name: Overgrown Slope

desc:
Sprawling masses of oak trees form a thick grove, their limbs sprouting out
leaves so dense they block out any incoming sunlight. Tall briar shrubs grow
between any open spaces their roots allow them to take. Little air moves through
this stale forest, making the environment stifling and humid.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Ancient oak trees grow tall in these woods, their massive branches overlapping
high in the air to create a dense roof of leaf, twig and vine. Thick and gnarled
roots grow outwards from the trees and crawl across the forest floor. Patches of
moist ground and mud collect in depressions and other low terrain, and thorny
shrubs sprout around these mud-pools.
name: Tangled Forest

desc:
The undergrowth here consists mostly of mud and trampled grass. Several trees,
oaks that stood for hundreds of years, lie uprooted in the mud, rotting away.
Great scratch marks mar the bark on several of the trees; both standing ones and
their less fortunate kin in the mud. Sticky, oozing vines hang from branches,
pale and sickly-looking, in stark contrast to the dark brown trunks.
name: Uprooted Hollow

desc:
A low but steeply sloping mountain range runs through Mirkwood here, offering a
view of the dense jungle-like woods below. Much of the ridge is tree-covered;
between trees spiny, sprawling thorn bushes grow wild. Black vines hang down
from the trees' branches, slithering on top of and through the bushes.
name: Ridge of Oaks

desc:
Large oaks grow at various angles on this ridge, their gnarled branches swaying
slowly in the breeze. The sides of the ridge fall away steeply but the ridge
itself is flat except for scattered, moss-covered rocks. A few black thorny
bushes grow in a cluster around a lone, large boulder.
name: Ridge of Oaks

desc:
Jagged rocks covered in a slimy moss cover this steep ridge; stunted oaks form
rows along the top of the ridge, sprouting from in-between rocks wherever there
is enough space. Branches intertwine above to form a dense, low-hanging canopy.
name: Ridge of Oaks

desc:
The terrain here is a sharp incline, offering a view over the surrounding
tangled woods. Large oak trees grow across the spine of the ridge; dark and
slight blackberry shrubs cover the steep slopes. A few large boulders entirely
covered in moss lie on the slopes where they blend in seamlessly with the
vegetation.
name: Ring of Trees

desc:
Rising out of the forest proper, this foothill of the surrounding mountains
includes an intact canopy of tall, black trees standing like watchtowers. The
ground slopes down to the east for relatively easy travel while to the south the
jumble of boulders and thick brush blocks passage.
name: Dark Foothill

desc:
A firm line of tall firs and dark oaks crowns this rocky area as the stony
foothills grow into small mountains to the west. The ground undulates as it
rises westward making travel fatiguing. Wet leaves and slippery verdigris mosses
cloak almost every exposed surface, adding to the treacherous passage.
name: Rising Mountains

desc:
The dark trees spread their reaching, grasping branches overhead, menacing
anyone who travels underneath. Tough, dark bushes spring up all around, making a
straight passage impossible, and to the west the face of a steep cliff cuts
through the forest. Frequently, small moss-covered rocks punch through the
leaf-covered ground.
name: Dangerous Woods

desc:
A sparse row of black oaks rise throughout these woods, beset on all sides by
large and thorny bramble patches. Tangled ivy crawls from bush to branch,
blanketing the vegetation. The dark, prickly brambles dominate the undergrowth,
scratching anyone who is bold enough to attempt the plunge through them.
name: Brambled Thicket

desc:
The conifer trees that once stood tall and proud in this small clearing have
been utterly destroyed, some even torn with their roots. It appears that the
bark on these fallen logs have been gnawed on by some large creatures. A foul
odour emanates from the soil, and the weeds and grass are stomped flat into the
stinky muck.
name: Smashed Coppice

desc:
The trees in this area are nearly obsidian in colour, the bark emanating a
darkness typical of these woods. Perhaps it is a trick of the light, of which
there is very little due to the thick and tangled canopy above. Black roots
crawl through the damp terrain around the oak trees, spreading across the forest
floor.
name: Dark Oaks

desc:
Dark and twisted oak trees grow thickly in these woods, thorny bushes scraping
against their bark. Vines crawl down the low hanging limbs touching the spiky
vegetation. The forest floor is teeming with dark grass; thick, gnarled roots
crawl through it. The dense canopy obstructs the view of the sky.
name: Gnarled Bosk

desc:
The air in these woods is stifling and humid. A thin mist rises between the
roots that crawl through the mud. Moss and a vast plethora of fungi sprout up
around the bases of the old twisted oaks. A patch of large, thorny bushes grows
in a dip of the terrain, their branches reaching up defiantly as if hoping to
get a fresh breeze by some miracle.
name: Musty Grove

desc:
The ground is cast in shadows as the crowns of the trees provide shade from sun-
and moonlight alike. A small dirty-looking pool has formed between some roots,
perhaps filled by the raindrops drizzling down the tops of trees. A few shrubs
sprout up along the edges of the pool, their leaves of dark, sickly green.
name: Shady Woods

desc:
Tall, ancient trees stand proudly amongst the tangled undergrowth, reaching up
towards the skies, their trunks holding up a vast, entangled canopy. A few large
rocks are piled up near the centre of a ring of oaks, covered in roots and
steeped in moss. Broad and black fallen leaves are scattered around the forest
floor.
name: Ancient Oaks

desc:
The trees in these parts of Mirkwood are stunted in their growth with wide,
gnarly trunks covered in coarse, mossy bark. Their branches twist and bend,
interlocking with each tree next to it in a tangled embrace. Their dark leaves
grow wide and flat and lichen dangles from the canopy like grasping grey-brown
tentacles.
name: Bent Forest

desc:
Tall pines grow here, shrouded in darkness. Black moss clings to the bark of the
trees and mottled grey-black vines hang down from their branches. Shaded bracken
grows thick around the trunks of the trees, thriving in the eternal gloom
beneath the canopy of Mirkwood.
name: Dark Pines

desc:
A ring of immense, gnarled black trees occupies this area, their branches
intertwining high above to form a dark canopy that casts a shadow over these
woods. The knotted roots of these towering trees spread out in a crooked
pattern, forming a sort of bowl on the forest floor. Water has collected in this
basin between the trees, dark and putrid from mingling with oozing sore-like
wounds of the roots.
name: Dark Pond

desc:
Squat, dark oak trees sprawl across the landscape with their branches drooping
toward the ground. Black vines dangle from the branches, reaching all the way
down to the forest floor, thus creating a shifting black-green curtain of
vegetation. Lichen grows on the vines, making them look like hairy tangled arms
grasping and dragging at the thorny briar shrubs that grow thickly amidst the
gnarled trees.
name: Twisted Oaks

desc:
The darkness envelops this area at all times of day; an overgrown and twisted
forest canopy spreads across the tree tops. Low-hanging branches tangle into one
another, connecting the trees to form walls of foliage that hinder travel in
several directions. The little grass growing here is dull and lifeless, and
overgrown with thorny weeds crawling their way across the terrain.
name: Dusky Copse

desc:
Thorny shrubbery dominates this landscape, growing wild and abundant in all
directions. The thorn-clad branches of these prickly bushes sway whenever the
slightest breeze blows through them. Many towering, dark trees stand defiant
among the briars, with dense foliage blocking the view to the sky.
name: Tangled Copse

desc:
Amidst the short and stocky trees of these surroundings some thick brambled
bushes grow, making access and travel very tedious and difficult. Thorny
branches scratch and drag at anything that moves along the forest floor and tree
roots crawl up through the soil. The branches stretch from tree to tree with
vines hanging down from them.
name: Overgrown Woodland

desc:
The vegetation in these woods grows wild and thick along a steep slope, causing
a difficult traverse through the landscape. Dark and tangled roots grow on the
forest floor beneath the twisted and stunted oaks; lichen hanging down from low
branches. A few moss-covered rocks are strewn across ground, with spiny
thornbushes cropping up in-between.
name: Overgrown Slope

desc:
The walls are crudely fashioned from a strong but plain digging technique that
simply shoveled unwanted rock and dirt out of the way. Claw and blade marks can
be seen biting into surfaces here and there. The dusty rock floor has been
disturbed recently as tracks come from the west yet the only entrance appears to
be from above. Trash and rotting debris decorate the edges of this space.
name: Underground Cavern

desc:
The rocky terrain slopes up and down, following the shape of a heavily forested
ridge that ascends to the west. Chunks of granite punch upwards between the huge
trees like stony fists and numerous moss-covered roots twist on the ground ready
to trip, grab and trap anyone coming too close to them. Overhead, dark branches
have intertwined to form a dense canopy.
name: Forested Mountains

desc:
A mountain ridge slowly rises to the west as the ground seems to deepen and
mature. Massive trees with dark trunks and occasional boulders precariously
perched atop rock walls line the ways leading north and south. The ground slopes
down to the east as it continues into a dark, forested area.
name: Rising Ridge

desc:
Loose rocks and hardy roots litter the rocky area, making walking on foot
difficult and unpleasant. The land slopes downhill to the east and north while a
steep drop to the south leads to dark, stony paths. Looking in most directions
presents an impressive view of Mirkwood. However, to the west, a rocky
protrusion lifts up and out to an even higher vantage point.
name: Rough Ridge

desc:
A faint trail can be followed, coming out of a ridge to the east and heading up
into a higher mountainous area. The way is narrow due to rock ledges and walls
on either side that seem to greedily want for attention. The rocky overhangs
block out most light and keep the area eternally dim.
name: Dark Path in the Mountains

desc:
The hilly forest slopes up and down, seemingly at random. Black pines rise from
the grey, crumbly rock to form a canopy that stretches in all directions,
filtering and blocking out most of the light. Several large, cracked boulders
lie on the forest floor.
name: Forested Slopes

desc:
The hills here have been taken over by thick stands of tall oak and birch that
spreads their branches above to form a dense canopy. The ground slopes downward
to the north and the forest floor gets increasingly wet further down. Some
plants like ferns and others that thrive in shade grow among a layer of leaves
that carpets the ground.
name: Overgrown Slopes

desc:
A row of thickly forested hills leans away from the mountain slopes to the
north. Ancient, sprawling trees and numerous boulders with moss-covered surfaces
cover the ground. Crowded by thick brush and dark weeds, passage is only
possible to the east and south.
name: Densely Wooded Hills

desc:
Thick, dark oaks and dense pines grow side by side like formations of marching
soldiers. Criss-crossed branches shoot down at all angles like crossed spears
and enormous, gnarled roots twist with stout vines to make passage impossible to
the north. The ground begins to slope to the west while to the south, dark
silence pervades the woods.
name: Guarded Woods

desc:
Huge, black oaks stand like sentinels, their great branches outstretched and
entangled so that no sunlight ever reaches the forest floor. Several trees have
fallen, effervescent mildew covering their rotting, corpse-like trunks. A haze
drifts in from the north making any kind of travel hazardous. The air is so
thick it feels like being underwater. On either side of a small worn trail,
ominous roots, covered with dead leaves, encourage passage through a large,
black pool of liquid that makes no movement, not even in the strongest wind.
name: Black Pool

desc:
A rather wide expanse of shale has been thrust upward like an outstretched arm
with the palm face-up. The result is a majestic, commanding view of the
surrounding areas: thickly forested Mirkwood to the west, dark hills to the
south, and darker patches of trees to the east. In the north, misty clouds seems
to permanently obscure any views in a thick haze.
name: Solitary Precipice

desc:
On all sides, the forest slopes down to a bowl-shaped valley, nestled deep
within the hills. Pierced by rocky outcroppings, the way back up is tangled with
jutting roots and hanging vines that seem ready to trap anyone fool enough to
try going back up. In the exact middle of the valley is a smooth rocky expanse
with no undergrowth on it.
name: Dark Valley in the Hills

desc:
Slick round stones form a peculiar pattern on the ground amidst long patches of
dried stiff thickets harbouring long, hollow thorns ending in needle-like
points. Great natural stone walls block most natural light from entering the
bottom of the chasm, and above, tall firs overshadow it. The walls are very
steep, save for one point where a narrow path leads upwards.
name: Bottom of a Chasm

desc:
Several clumps of dirt and small, round stones litter the bottom of the chasm,
which is framed by smooth, tall cliffs that impede natural light, only to be
broken up by a small opening to the west. Dry, green-brown shrubs, some over two
feet high, grow on the bottom of the chasm, the sole vegetation to be found.
name: Bottom of a Chasm

desc:
Solid ground ends abruptly here and the very edge of it displays many cracks in
the earth with obvious spots where the earth has crumbled and fallen into the
rift below. Some of these cracks feature small grey roots growing out of them,
part of the cause of the deterioration of the soil. One particular root is a few
feet long and hangs over the edge, as if it was dragged down by something.
name: Crumbling Edge of a Chasm

desc:
Oak and fir trees seem to mesh with one another in a small wooded valley, the
branches and leaves overlapping to create a blur of dark vegetation and
blackness beyond. Thorny vines and bristly roots cover the forest floor under
the shadow of the great trees looming above.
name: Forest in a Small Valley

desc:
The canopy and entwined branches of the dark and gnarled trees keep this area of
the valley eternally dark. Gigantic black roots spread out like cobwebs from the
feet of what must be extremely ancient oaks. A wrathful anger is almost
palpable, seeming to emanate from the hateful trees that stand tall and ruthless
above the inhospitable ground beneath them.
name: Sinister Forest

desc:
Old, gnarled trees form a natural barrier as their branches wrap around one
another, twisting into a complex bark-covered mesh. A small puddle has collected
in a depression in the ground, made up of murky black water that continues to
drip from the overhanging trees. Boulders appear to have tumbled down from the
mountains that rise to the west and south, coming to rest violently in the soft
earth beneath the tall trees amid their ruthless strangling roots.
name: Warped Wooded Depression

desc:
Immensely tall, pitch black oaks tower over the stifled dusty ground below. The
sharp ridged bark of the merciless trees seems to carve its way out of the
rot-filled soil, littered with the remnants of smaller vegetation that partially
wasted away long ago but that never fully decayed. The thickness of the great
black roots that burst forth from the rancid ground make even some of the
broader branches look thin by comparison.
name: Blackened Forest

desc:
A great collection of crudely-hewn stumps jut up from the mountainous ground
among many large rocks that lie interspersed with the few fir trees that still
grow in this part of Mirkwood. Some of the stones are blackened with ash, and
the surrounding woods seem to produce a feeling of tenseness. The air is stuffy
and thick with malice.
name: Burnt Mountain Forest

desc:
Few trees remain of what was once undoubtedly a densely forested mountainside.
Numerous trees appear to have been crudely hacked down and limbed here. Evidence
of a recent campfire is apparent, with charred wood in a pile nestled under an
ash-covered boulder. Other blackened rocks are visible with similar traces,
appearing as though they too had been exposed to a recent fire.
name: Scorched Rocks and Hewn Stumps

desc:
Hulking twisting tree trunks interlock with great gnarled boughs and branches to
form an inhospitable wasteland beneath the black lightless canopy of Mirkwood.
Long murderous vines squeeze both root and trunk, sapping life from every living
thing that they touch. Crusted lichen dangles from the serrated bark of the
towering trees, while wicked thorn-filled brush chokes out all other vegetation
from the forest floor.
name: Thorn-choked Wooded Wasteland

desc:
Down in a shallow crack of the forest floor lies a rugged miniature canyon
covered entirely over its top by thick tangled vines. The vines grow so numerous
here that they themselves block all light that might have reached the bottom of
the crack, twisting this way and that along the stone walls as they throttle all
other living things.
name: Vine-covered Crevice

desc:
Loose slimy rocks and sprawling roots cover the ground beneath the sea of trees
that loom dark and ominously above. Shiny, slippery portions of wet roots
protrude at various points from the dark moist soil. The ground becomes
extremely steep to the north as it climbs toward the peaks of the Mountains of
Mirkwood.
name: Rough Mountain Terrain

desc:
Every manner of parasitic vine and creeper have entirely overtaken the forest
here via strangulation and murderous sap-sucking conquest. Thick, gnarled bodies
of strangely long, agile tentacles have forced their way under root and over
boulder, choking all other life from this place in a ruthless and magnificent
display of tangled tortuous takeover.
name: Tangled Nest of Vines

desc:
Dark, enveloping coniferous trees peer over the rising mountain slope facing the
south. Great bulbous root balls grow precariously atop sprawling stone slabs
beneath the menacing trees. Smooth, rocky cliffs form an impassable line to the
east while to the west and north, the forest climbs upward with the mountain.
name: Rising Mountain Slope

desc:
Gloomy pine trees with cold, dry needles grow here atop the lower Mountains of
Mirkwood. Stunted bushes and vegetation struggle to survive in the darkness on
the cold stoney ground. Each tree has broken its way through a tough layer of
bedrock as it took root in some miniscule crack long ago, splitting the stone as
it aged over countless years.
name: Mountainous Forest

desc:
The steep slope is covered with massive boulders that form small crawl spaces
beneath and around various stones, making up a network of tiny tunnels and
crevices in the darkness beneath the canopy of the forest. A large sprawling
field of sharp rocks covers much of the soil around the bases of the trees,
indicating that a landslide occurred here in the recent past.
name: Steep Mountainside

desc:
Small scraggly shrubs grow among withered fir trees that overlook a massive
cliff to the west. Dark boughs loom high above the steep rocky soil surrounding
one great stone peak that rises up into the dense canopy, disappearing in its
dark tangled mass.
name: Forest Peak in the Mountains

desc:
The denseness of the forest even on this lower peak ensures that not a single
ray of sunlight reaches the rocky ground. Towering trees rise upward from steep
mountainsides with their massive roots brutally plunging deep into the stone as
they seek sustenance and stability. Small shrubs cling precariously to steep
stone faces, their roots visible in the small cracks and fissures along the
bedrock.
name: Forested Mountain Peak

desc:
A dry cavern formed inside the hard granite mountainside has been altered to
serve as a modest abode. A door crafted specifically to remain hidden from the
outside adorns the southern wall, though the rest of the cave appears to formed
naturally. The cave continues in darkness to the north and east.
name: Dry Cave

desc:
A vast natural cavern has formed here with a tall ceiling supported by thick
columns of pure granite. The smooth stone suggests that water flowed through
here at one time, though the cavern is entirely dry and appears as though it has
remained absent of moisture for many years. The floor is worn smooth, tilting
slightly upward toward the north which disappears into further darkness.
name: Large Cavern

desc:
Uneven stone surfaces and sudden dark crevices with no bottom in sight fill this
cavern drowned in darkness. Strange stone steppes climb up in seemingly
arbitrary locations, only to fall steeply down below the floor. Sharp ridges of
splintered rock jut up from the uneven floor in various locations, while dark
alcoves and corners lead to dead-ends along the northern and western walls.
name: Under the Mountains

desc:
A large alcove is tucked back in the corner of the larger cave system, and it is
currently serving as a home for some very large humanoids. The alcove, which
would be considered a modest-sized cavern to most, is adorned with a massive
stone table that sits in the centre of the room. The inhabitants of this humble
abode appear to live modestly, not caring for subtle pleasantries normally found
in most dwellings.
name: Giant's Home

desc:
Clumps of withered pines grow here amidst a grove of stumps that have clearly
been hacked apart by axes. Many large rocks cover the ground, covered in
blackened ash and other signs of recent campfires. To the north thick foliage
dominates the forest floor between massive severed tree trunks.
name: Chopped Grove

desc:
Tall dark tree trunks crowd each other on the hillsides in every direction
surrounding a sudden depression that cleaves its way down into the earth. The
cavern opening appears as a black maw, even in the darkness of the thick forest
canopy that prevents natural light from penetrating to the uneven ground below.
name: Hillside Cavern Entrance

desc:
This cavern descends away from a cave mouth to the south. The western side is
lower than the eastern, a continuation of the slanting entrance. Narrow crevices
in the deeper part of the cavern are filled with brackish, silty water. One of
these crevices cuts into the western wall, where it opens into a broad pool.
name: Tilted Tunnel

desc:
A dark pool covers the entire floor of a high-ceilinged chamber. The pool is
shallow in various places where sand bars rise to the surface. Deep crevices
filled with opaque water make it difficult to discern the difference between
shallow and deep areas. The stagnant oily water is occasionally disturbed by
bubbles that ominously rise to the surface from unknown depths below. A gap in
the eastern wall leads to a north-south passage that slants upward away from the
pool.
name: Silty Underground Pool

desc:
This north-south passage slants steeply across its axis. The high side along the
eastern wall is relatively level, making for sound footing. The passage falls
away from this natural path, narrowing eventually into waterlogged crevices. A
few shattered and rotten logs lie in the deep side of the passage.
name: Crooked Creviced Passage

desc:
This passage has an oblong cross section which is slightly tilted so that the
eastern end is higher than the western. Jagged cracks crisscross the walls and
ceiling of the passage, none more than several inches wide. Water seeps from the
cracks, running westward down the slanted passage and into a wide gap that seems
to broaden as it disappears into the ground.
name: Slanted Passage

desc:
Several dark, forbidding openings lead away from this great intersection. Some
of the exits are small, no larger than crawlspaces. Others are so great that an
Oliphant could stand in them. While the cavern's origin is no doubt natural, it
is now the habitat of creatures that dump their refuse in piles along the walls
and create paths that lead to and from the various tunnels.
name: Expansive, Intersected Cavern

desc:
A large natural cavern is worn into the bedrock here by untold years of flowing
water. Frequently used paths are clearly marked on the floor leading off in all
directions, while refuse and unidentifiable junk is scattered in piles along the
walls. A low opening that is rounded and much smoother than the surrounding rock
allows passage to the south.
name: Expansive Cavern

desc:
A cavern opens into a large circular grotto. A set of simple, crude stairs leads
down into a deeper tunnel below. Though this part of the cave may have been
natural in origin, rough work has clearly made the place more habitable. Large
stalagmites have been broken off and ground down to serve as smooth table tops.
The floor appears fairly level, and many of the smaller stone formations have
been hewn away.
name: Atop Simple Stone Steps

desc:
This part of the cavern descends sharply from its entrance to the south,
flattening out into a broad chamber pocked with shallow pools. Water trickles
down the walls and drips slowly from the ceiling. Piles of bedding lie scattered
in the dry spots between pools. Each pool seems filled with sundry pieces of
garbage, filling the air with the stench of rot.
name: Damp Barracks

desc:
The walls of this enormous tunnel-like cavern are lined with deep cracks,
outcroppings, and fallen scree. At a section of the western wall, which is
remarkably smooth compared to the rest of the cavern, a narrow opening leads
into an alcove. There are trampled paths along the cavern floor leading north,
south and into the alcove.
name: Great Cavern by an Alcove

desc:
Jumbled piles of miscellaneous supplies fill a large alcove connected to vast
caverns to the east. Towers of half-full, opened crates sit near the entrance,
their supplies spilling onto the ground. Empty crates line the wall to the west,
tossed in a great heap lacking any sort of order. A layer of sawdust, splinters,
and large planks covers the floor.
name: Cluttered Alcove

desc:
The tall stone walls veer outward here to form a truly massive unlit cavern. The
ceiling is hidden in a shroud of gloom overhead and standing against one wall
reveals nothing about the other side of the cave except blackness. The ground is
surprisingly soft, being apparently made from layers of gravel and sand as well
as mud toward the centre of the cave. The walls narrow to the south, as if much
of the had water drained away into the bedrock.
name: Vast Cavern

desc:
In a slow majestic bend, the enormous cavern wraps itself around a rough wall of
granite to the south-east. The smooth, wet stone floor continues down into the
darkness as it carries on to the south. Shallow pools of dark water along the
centre of the cave, coupled with patterns in the sand, seem to indicate that
water occasionally flows through here.
name: Sloping Cavern

desc:
Much of the ceiling of this vast cavern is lost in gloom. Sharp, glittering tips
of stalactites sprout from the darkness in various locations as water slowly
drips from their tips to land on the cavern's wet floor. The ground slopes from
east to west, showing signs that water recently flowed here. Though the cave
appears natural in its formation, a well-trampled path follows the lowest point.
Small pieces of refuse litter the ground along the subterranean trail.
name: Vast, Natural Cavern

desc:
Scraggly trees rise upward toward the forest canopy above the entrance to an
enormous cavern. Massive stalactites hang down from the low arch of the opening
itself which, together with the trees growing through the stone lip at the
bottom of the entrance appear to form a great gaping maw. The cavern itself
stretches away westward, fading into deep shadow.
name: Broad Cavern Entrance

desc:
The thick, tangled forested hills crowd up against an enormous cavern entrance
leading down into complete darkness. Great trunks rise from the very mouth of
the cave opening, making the pitch black entrance appear even darker than the
rest of the gloomy wood. The soil becomes softer as the ground slopes downward
into the stoney recess.
name: Tangled Hillside Forest at a Cavern

desc:
Tall trees blot out the sky, leaving the hilly terrain below in a heavy
darkness. The tangly roots that emanate from every trunk create large mounds of
tough, gnarled wooden loops that twist and turn into one another. A mist of
smelly spores hang in the air produced by pitcher-like black fungi that have
overrun the forest floor.
name: Looming Wooded Foothills

desc:
Mighty trees cover the rising hills in a complete darkness, dominating the
landscape in every direction. Gnarled oaks and disfigured pines brood in the
dense air, stifling any drafts that might be blowing above the canopy. Thick
leaves cover the ground around the great trunks rising from their tough, knotted
root foundations.
name: Towering Wooded Hillside

desc:
A wide, rugged shelf clings to the western side of the rising Emyn-nu-Fuin. The
hills jut sharply upward at the eastern edge but fall away on the western side
just as abruptly. The shelf is littered with large, moss-covered boulders and
jagged outcroppings clad in grey lichen. Ancient trees spread their wide
branches overhead, obscuring the skies beneath a great green ceiling.
name: Densely Forested Shelf

desc:
Steep cliffs rise to the east and south, closing the forested shelf in on two
sides. A steep slope extends away westward, covered in tangled thorny bushes and
great black oaks. The shelf itself is primarily flat and dotted with large mossy
boulders. Thousands of large, black butterflies flutter about just above the
crowns of the ancient oaks growing on the slope to the west.
name: Dark Woods on a Shelf

desc:
This cavern is wide and round, though the ceiling is fairly low throughout. The
floor is made up of rough stone and is covered in crude bedding made from
tattered furs, skins, and pelts. A foul musk fills the air and the stale odour
of sweat mingled with a damp rot emanates from the bedding. The ceiling rises
gently to the immediate north where this chamber joins with a much larger
cavern.
name: Low Barracks

desc:
A gloomy passageway carved from the dark bedrock begins here at the foot of a
set of crude stairs to the west. The top of the stairs is shrouded in darkness
and of the passageway that leads off towards the east no more than a few dozen
feet can be seen before it disappears into inky blackness. Some care was taken
when crafting this tunnel, for while the walls and ceiling are uneven and rough,
the floor is flat and smooth, making for easy travel.
name: Sombre Passageway

desc:
A crude passageway cuts through black shale as it runs east-to-west. Chambers
branch off north and south, their contents hidden in gloomy shadow. A foul
medley of odours lingers in this tunnel, including an acrid burnt smell mixed
with the dank orkish musk and a faint tinge of rot.
name: Juncture in a Sombre Passageway

desc:
This oblong chamber is dozens of yards long east-to-west, yet only at most three
yards wide north to south. A narrow gap in the northern wall serves as the only
exit and the only means for a strong and acrid odour to escape into the tunnels
beyond. Cobwebs cover the walls but it is quite clear that the chamber itself
sees daily use.
name: Bare Sleeping Chamber

desc:
Paint-flecked bones hang from the walls and ceiling of a low chamber underground
that is shrouded in complete blackness. A crude wooden table covered in
miscellaneous herbs, animal entrails and strange tools sits at the centre of the
cave, while a recess deep in the eastern wall contains a pot suspended over a
pile of black coals and ashes. The air is slightly hazy and contains an acrid
odour interspersed with the stench of recent death.
name: Smoky Chamber

desc:
Hulking tree trunks support a looming canopy of thick, dark vegetation making it
impossible to discern night from day. Tough sinewy vines slither their way among
branch and bough as they ruthlessly seek out further sustenance in their overtly
parasitic fashion. Great roots jut from the uneven hilly forest floor as they
continue their own search for water in the rocky soil below.
name: Elevated Forest

desc:
The forest is dominated by ancient sprawling oaks armoured by thick, ridged bark
that spirals down to each tree's rooted feet. Thorny vines and black-leafed
creepers snake upward around the knotted trunks, disappearing into the dense
canopy above. Ferns and fungi thrive on the shaded forest floor amidst a thick
layer of fallen leaves that covers the ground.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
Moulds grow in a slithering, oozing pattern down twisted tree trunks in this
hilly region of Mirkwood. Tangled vines weave their way around thorny bushes,
creating choked undergrowth filled with perilous obstacles and razor sharp
thorn-filled thickets. A faint, diffused green glow makes up the only light that
naturally reaches the forest floor during daylight hours.
name: Hilly Forest

desc:
Mixed oak and fir trees grow interspersed atop rolling hills while small shrubs
with withered leaves waste away on the forest floor. Dark stone slabs protruding
through the topsoil combine with the rotting plant matter to blacken the
hillsides beneath the looming trees that cover them.
name: Blackened Forested Hills

desc:
The forest covers rough, mountainous terrain as the slopes of Emyn-nu-Fuin grow
steeper. Large blackened boulders dot the climbing landscape amid countless
twisted trees, each harbouring its own strange perforations in its bark and
lower limbs. The rocky soil supports various small bushes even on its steeper
slopes beneath the mighty oaks and firs that race upward into the dense canopy.
name: Rugged Mountain Forest

desc:
These rocky outcrops are shaded by tall, hoary trees. A canopy of tree limbs
covered with grey lichen obstructs the view of the sky. Cracked boulders sit
between the outcrops, perhaps separated from the crags by the action of wind and
rain. Vines and roots now intertwine with these boulders, continuing the process
of breaking them down into dirt.
name: Shadowed Crags

desc:
Massive, black oak trees loom to either side of a rugged ridge, their gnarled
and sweeping limbs hanging low to the ground. Bone white vines tangle with the
branches, wrapping around the massive trunks, cracked and weeping a dark and
foul smelling sap. Dense shrubs surround boulders atop the ridge.
name: Overgrown Ridge

desc:
Gnarled and twisted trees grow closely together in a large grove, their branches
intertwined and entangled. Black moss grows along the bark of the trees and
spreads up to the undersides of their branches. A riot of thorn-covered bushes
grows in the shade beneath the canopy, seemingly thriving in the light-starved
conditions.
name: Prickly Grove

desc:
Squat, gnarled oaks press close on all sides, leaving only narrow tracts of
passable ground between them. The thick, knotted branches of the trees droop
low, covered in a dark, moist lichen. The lichen seems to creep along the bark
whenever a stifled breeze manages to find its way through the suffocating
canopy.
name: Gloomy Oaks

desc:
The woods grow thick among these crags, and cast dark shadows on the boulder-
strewn forest floor. The air is thick with smells of decay and rot, and several
trees lie uprooted and broken on the ground. Black vines crawl across tree limbs
in a tangled mess, intertwining to form the dark canopy overhead.
name: Shadowed Crags

desc:
The surroundings become increasingly craggy toward the apex of this rugged
ridgeline to the west. Vegetation in the form of hoary, ancient trees, sprawling
thornbushes, and plants of all sorts cover the mountains. A long, forested slope
descends into a valley to the north. The valley floor is shrouded by dense
foliage and nothing of it can be seen.
name: Shadowed Crags

desc:
Heavily forested, rocky, and craggy ground forms a series of ridges that expand
in all directions. The cliffs to the east are steep and cut short any thought of
travel in that direction. The undergrowth is made up of moss and ferns, with
some larger bushes growing along the edges of narrow ravines where the canopy is
less dense.
name: Shadowed Crags

desc:
Ancient oaks cover these hills entirely, their green canopies blotting out the
sky. A variety of sickly-looking fungi grows on their trunks, on boulders,
roots, and the forest floor. These fungi are black and grey, and a rotten smell
rises from a yellow liquid slowly oozing from them to gather in repugnant pools
on the ground.
name: Shadowed Crags

desc:
Low, rugged mountains rise above Mirkwood. The terrain is rough with jutting
rocks, moss-covered boulders, and small gorges everywhere. Trees with black bark
and knotted roots rise among the boulders to spread their crowns above, casting
the mountains into gloom.
name: Shadowed Crags

desc:
Struggling, sickly trees blanket the slopes of broad, low hills. Their branches
droop to the ground, their wilting leaves spotted with black. The trunks of the
trees are covered in yellow-green fungi which cloud the air with choking spores.
The putrid odour, the mottled bark, and the sagging crowns of the trees combine
to tell a story of rot and decay.
name: Foul, Forested Foothills

desc:
A wide variety of trees and bushes grows here, all crowded along the bottom of a
long, narrow pass. The sides of the pass rise almost vertically, with some
jagged outcroppings and small ledges here and there. On these some small weeds
and moss have found a home.
name: Overgrown Pass

desc:
A series of passes allow for fairly comfortable passage through this part of the
Emyn-nu-Fuin. Some trees grow here, competing for domination with black, spindly
thorn bushes and all kinds of weeds. Dead vegetation has gathered at the lowest
depressions in the passes, creating shallow swamp-like pools overgrown with
fungi.
name: Thorny Junction

desc:
As the vegetation and trees begin to recede, the terrain rises above the
surrounding landscape. Spiny thorn bushes grow among rocks and stones, thriving
even in the shade of large, moss-covered boulders. Black patch of bramble grows
along the floor of this ridge, thorns pointing outward in defence.
name: Thorny Ridge

desc:
Ancient oaks grow on the slopes and across the top of this ridge. Their wide
crowns seem to reach for each other, as if trying to create a canopy over the
ridge. A wide array of vegetation including berry bushes and thorny brambles
thrives in the gaps between trees, where sun and rain can reach the forest floor
unhindered.
name: Forested Ridge

desc:
The forest floor is covered with rocky outcroppings and leaves fallen from the
tremendous black oaks of this mountainous region of Mirkwood. Thick, thorny
vines climb trunks, circling round and round as if trying to choke the trees.
Branch, leaf, and vine conspire to create a canopy, yet it is not dense enough
to hide the sky from view.
name: Tangled Slope

desc:
Deep ruts have been formed in varying patterns on the ground here as a product
of erosion and water flowing down from higher ground. Between these cracks,
plant life has taken advantage of the cool shade and grows in abundance. One
species of thorn bushes seems to thrive unlike any other plant. Their long,
spiny branches scrape and scratch anything that comes too close to their domain.
name: Raveled Mesa

desc:
The canting nature of the terrain might be what causes the trees to grow twisted
and bent in odd angles. Vines twist around the trees, covering their bark like
thorny, tangled snakes. A foul and stinking black moss grows across the rocks
and boulders. The slope climbs into a mountain pass to the west, while
descending into a hilly area to the east.
name: Overgrown Slope

desc:
The narrow, twisting mountain path follows the shape of the mountains. The
steep, rugged walls of natural rock severely limit the options of directions to
travel to. Black moss clings to the mountain face, coating sections in its
darkness. Some vegetation grows even in this desolate area, chiefly tough weeds
and the occasional thorny bush.
name: Narrow Mountain Pass

desc:
A narrow mountain pass forges a path through these forerunners of the
Emyn-nu-Fuin. A few thin oaks grow amidst the rubble and bushes that line the
pass on either side. The oaks are feeble specimens of their kind, with spindly
branches and narrow trunks. Sometimes howling winds chase through the pass,
bouncing between steep walls as they fills the air with dust.
name: Narrow Mountain Pass

desc:
A large moss-covered boulder stands squat in the middle of the mountain pass,
with overgrown bushes on one side and a twisted oak tree on the other one. The
gnarled branches of the tree, draped in clinging black vines, scrape against the
boulder. The surrounding terrain is arid and sparsely vegetated, though tough
weeds seem to find the area agreeable.
name: Narrow Mountain Pass

desc:
Large, black bushes cover this mountain pass. Their thorny branches are clad in
small and round green leaves. A few tall alders stand on the steep slopes, and
fungi thrive in their shadows rather than the thorn bushes growing everywhere
else. While the slopes to either side are very steep, rocky and treacherous, the
floor of the pass is flat and not difficult to travel upon.
name: Narrow Mountain Pass

desc:
To the south the terrain rises towards a narrow mountain pass that runs between
steep cliff walls. Large boulders and small rocks form a rough and rocky floor
covered with dark roots and thorny brambles. The occasional black oak grows on
the sheer mountain face, limbs slowly swaying in a foul breeze.
name: Narrow Mountain Pass

desc:
The terrain becomes very steep towards the north where a slope climbs towards
higher ground; to the west, another short but steep slope beckons. Great sprawls
of thorny bushes cover the gentler parts of the slope, and in-between pebbles
and small rocks make footing unsure. A few trees dot the area, sparse enough not
to be called a proper forest.
name: Overgrown Slope

desc:
A wide, rugged ridge stretches from the east to the west, while a gentle slope
descends to the south. The terrain makes for very difficult travel, with crags
and cliffs everywhere. Gnarled, twisted trees rise from in-between rocks and
boulders. Although stunted and bent, they hold up massive, sprawling crowns that
form a dense canopy overhead. On the ground, little but moss and ferns seem to
find the dark, stony environment agreeable.
name: Shadowed Crags

desc:
Moss-covered crags and gnarled, ancient trees dominate this region of the
Emyn-nu-Fuin. Layers of dead leaves cover a forest floor shrouded in gloom by
dense foliage above. Half-buried rocks and thick, knotted roots hide under the
leaves. Dense, thorny patches of briar grow in clusters wherever there is a gap
in the canopy.
name: Shadowed Crags

desc:
Jagged boulders and deep, narrow gorges await the unwary traveller in the
shadows under the trees in this part of the Emyn-nu-Fuin. Grey lichen drapes
branches that droop as if the flimsy fungi were of great weight. The forest
floor dips sharply to the west, the rocky terrain disappearing from view beneath
dark canopies.
name: Shadowed Crags

desc:
The steep rocky terrain rises a hundred feet above the forest. These low, rugged
mountains host a plethora of plant life, ranging from moss and small shrubs to
tall, ancient trees. Steep cliffs rise to the west, separating this part of the
Emyn-nu-Fuin from whatever lies on the other side.
name: Tangled Mountain Range

desc:
The terrain ascends from all directions to reach this flat plateau. The ground
is stony with little topsoil and what vegetation grows here is made up of small
shrubs and plants. Several large boulders dot the plateau, moss-covered looming
giants that stand guard over their domain.
name: Wide Plateau

desc:
Sharp and jagged rocks covered in slimy green moss protrude from a broad ridge
that runs from the east to the west. Twisted trees flank the apex of the ridge,
casting shadows on it with their overhanging branches. Dark brown vines hang
from the trees, and roots push their way up through the crumbled rocks atop the
ridge.
name: Overgrown Ridge

desc:
The dark trees twist and stretch into a warped tangle of gnarled boughs and
mossy limbs. No natural light pierces the gloom of the treetops, as evidenced by
a complete lack of vegetation beneath the canopy. Ominous roots weave their way
through the thin soil, becoming a tangled net of hardened wood and scaly bark.
name: Gloomy Forested Hill

desc:
Short and spiny thorn bushes cover these low hills, growing thick and wild. A
few elm trees stand defiant along the ridges, their arms swaying slowly
seemingly independent of the wind; faint, rustling sounds are ever present. The
dense vegetation is covered with a parasitic black moss that emanates a foul
odour that carries through the whole area.
name: Brushwood Hills

desc:
Thorny barberry bushes grow thick on the rubble-like hillsides. There is a faint
trail worn into the crab grass between the bushes. The trees that grow on the
floor between hills seem to be mostly gnarled ancients. Sometimes a faint
howling rides on the wind from the north, though if it is caused by air being
forced through narrow gaps and openings or something else is indeterminable.
name: Brushwood Hills

desc:
The air is thick with the heavy musk of trolls. The rock underfoot has been
scraped smooth by the frequent shuffling of immense beings. The walls bear
jagged claw-marks while even the tall ceiling shows signs of having been gashed.
A darkly-stained path leads to the west.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
Dark and musty, this cave is little more than a turn in a larger passageway.
Littered with rock shards and decaying matter, the floor is somewhat tricky to
navigate in a rush. Ancient cobwebs shroud the high ceiling corners in dark
shadows. The clearest path through here bends to the west and to the south.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
Dark and musty, this cave is little more than a turn in a larger passageway.
Littered with rock shards and decaying matter, the floor is somewhat tricky to
navigate in a rush. Ancient cobwebs shroud the high ceiling corners in dark
shadows. The clearest path through here bends to the south and to the east.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
The air is thick with the heavy musk of trolls. The rock underfoot has been
scraped smooth by the frequent shuffling of immense beings. The walls bear
jagged claw-marks while even the tall ceiling shows signs of having been gashed.
A darkly-stained path leads to the east.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
Dark and musty, this cave is little more than a turn in a larger passageway.
Littered with rock shards and decaying matter, the floor is somewhat tricky to
navigate in a rush. Ancient cobwebs shroud the high ceiling corners in dark
shadows. The clearest path through here bends to the north and to the east.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
The scent of trolls is thick in the air here and this cavern shows signs of
frequent passage by immense creatures. Clawed, ripped, and torn, the hard rock
surfaces of the walls, floors, and ceiling reveal abuse by furious powers. An
almost tube-like opening heads east while to the north and west, an ominous
darkness hangs over jagged passageways.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
This east-west passageway is a hastily dug tunnel that appears to receive
frequent use, judging by the claw marks in the floor. Roughly carved in a tube,
this hollow shaft connects the dank, foetid caves in the west with the black
caverns to the east.
name: Dimly-Lit Tunnel

desc:
The cavern widens out tremendously here in all directions. Far off to the east,
west, and north are dark openings that look like passageways. The ground is
cracked and jagged as old debris, rock shards, and bits of bone festoon the
area. A trail of this garbage seems to lead to the eastward opening.
name: Tunnel End

desc:
This area is nothing more than a big hole dug in the hard rock and then filled
with layer upon layer of discarded bones, clothing, broken weapons and armour,
and any other junk that trolls find no use for. Over time, the layers have
become several tens of feet deep and consequently exude the very essence of the
aroma of rot and decay.
name: Garbage Pit

desc:
A subtle but deep red glow suffuses this cavernous space. The air is warmer here
as heat rises from deep fissures near the far walls. Shadows cast by crystalline
stalagmites play tricks on the eyes as the faint sound of hissing and bubbling
can be heard deep below. Overhead, intricate stalactite formations catch and
reflect the illumination causing the entire chamber to wash in hues of rubies
and fire.
name: Crystalline Cavern

desc:
The ceiling soars tremendously high in the cavern, opening up the space like a
dark cathedral. Exits lead in all directions and it is clear from the trampled
floor that this crossing receives a lot of traffic. In addition to the massive
footprints, it is also evident that something or someone has been dragged across
the floor, leaving a bloody trail that heads east.
name: Cavernous Crossing

desc:
Fairly regular in appearance, this connecting tunnel has been dug to allow swift
passage for large beings, if the height of the ceilings are any clue. Old
bloodstains mar the walls while high up, ancient cobwebs drape down in dusty,
furry loops. The dusty floor has been disturbed very recently in this dank and
dark tunnel.
name: Dank Tunnel

desc:
Rough strength appears to have ripped open the rockfaces to the east and west in
the semblance of a fork in a path. To the west, silence and darkness pervade a
moist passageway while to the east, the cave seems drier but no less dark. To
the south, a long dark tunnel extends into the darkness.
name: Rough Fork

desc:
This small cave feels tight and compressed since the ceiling dips low while the
floor rises slightly. The granite walls bulge inward somewhat as the path
leading in dwindles and dies on the western side of the chamber. The air feels
heavy and warm with the musk of trolls and cave beasts.
name: Crowded Cave

desc:
This dark offshoot of a subtly glowing cavern in the east has been carved in
what looks like the design of a nest or a small den. Rank droppings and bits of
slimy flesh mixed with bone layer the floor. The walls are rough, uneven, and
might, from the look of them, have been excavated with claws rather than tools.
name: Dank Grotto

desc:
The passages from the west and north tighten as the path takes a sharp turn. The
hard-edged rock walls do not look friendly and the ceiling seems to be pointing
needle-like fingers as dark stalactites festoon its surface. The rank air feels
compressed and crowded here.
name: Sharp Turn

desc:
This stony path is little more than a tight squeeze between rock walls as it
twists from south to east. A faint rushing or hissing sound can be heard farther
east as the air begins to cool slightly. The dirty floor looks like it's hardly
been used though it looks ancient. Cobwebs drape the corners in shrouds of pale
lace, gently pulsing in a faint breeze.
name: Twisting Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel twists from west to south and a slow draft moves the air. A faint
hissing sound comes from somewhere to the south. The rock surfaces are slightly
damp and their rough edges glisten. The hardpacked floor is too damp to be
dusty; however, signs of use are still evident as the way slopes downward from
north to south.
name: Rough Bend

desc:
The passage from the north opens into a large cave with a high ceiling covered
in glistening stalactites. Each one is intermittently dripping moisture as a
wave of cool, moist air continuously flows in from the south. From that
direction comes the sound of rushing water. The rock floor is wet and slippery
as it dips down to the south.
name: Moist Cave

desc:
A cavern here has been cut naturally over the centuries by an underground spring
that enters high in the southern wall and falls down into a deep pool of black
water. A rocky ledge meanders along the western side of the pool and appears to
go through and beyond the waterfall's shimmering curtain.
name: Underground Waterfall

desc:
A ledge from the north descends into the dark pool created by constantly flowing
water. The water flows in from the north and through the ages it has carved a
passage deep beneath Mirkwood. The water finds an outlet somewhere beneath the
black surface, for the constant inflow does not raise the surface.
name: Deep, Dark Pool

desc:
The rough, shard-littered floor of the rest of this dark cavern gives way to a
pitch-black beach at the edge of an even blacker lake. Smooth and slick, the
shore slopes down to the water's edge where silent ripples gently lap against
the dark stone. The far side of the lake is impossible to gauge in any kind of
light but the water's movement suggests it is not devoid of some sort of life.
name: Dark Beach

desc:
A lake of black water fills the lower half of a vast cavern. The lake is deep
enough that the bottom is out of reach except within a few feet of the steeply
sloped beach. Warm currents seem to swish around in eddies giving rise to the
possibility that there is more than just darkness in these waters.
name: Black Lake

desc:
Dim light reaches this cavern from the west, though not enough to clearly
illuminate it or dispel the shadows that lurk in the corners. Even when the
simple door is closed, the faint glow is visible through the cracks around it.
Like an afterthought, this roughly-hewn space is a possible nesting site or
resting place with no room for large groups. On the eastern side of the room, a
giant crack in the floor is covered in moisture from some source deep below.
name: Damp Cave

desc:
This cave passage slopes upward to the east while to the west it descends into
gloom and darkness. The walls have been roughly hewn out of the black granite
and the floor is dusty from frequent use. The rank odour of trolls and rotten
meat fills the air.
name: Black Granite Cavern

desc:
A squat, square chamber sits at the intersection of two passageways. The larger
runs east-to-west and is carved straight and smooth. The other passage runs
north-to-south, and is crudely delved with uneven walls as well as a rough
floor. This chamber appears to be used as a guard post; wooden fortifications
line the west end of the cavern.
name: Guard Post in a Sombre Passageway

desc:
A wide passageway cutting through grey-black shale meets a massive wooden
portcullis guarding the way east. Natural rock formations line the walls and
ceiling, but the floor has been worn smooth. The passage is increasingly covered
in dust and small stones as it approaches the portcullis; beyond it to the east,
visible through the thick hardwood lattice, lie large mounds of debris.
name: Sombre Passageway before a Broken Portcullis

desc:
A spacious chamber far underground appears to serve as a well-stocked armoury
for the orkish forces that dwell here. A collection of brutal weapons hang on
pegs along the walls and stand in wooden racks that rest along the floor in a
ring surrounding the centre of the cavern. Crudely fashioned leather armour
hangs from pegs and is visible within various crates that appear to have
recently been smashed open and then hastily nailed back together by eager
occupants.
name: Well-furnished Armoury

desc:
An underground chamber that was rudely cut from solid rock shows few signs that
attention was paid to aesthetics. The walls and floor are rough and uneven, and
a large pile of fine furs and pelts lies strewn across the western half of the
chamber. Dilapidated leather armour, broken wooden shields, and shoddy stone
weapons litter the rest of the floor. A barren patch of red-stained stone sits
beneath a pair of empty shackles mounted to the eastern wall.
name: Chieftain's Quarters

desc:
Large boulders and jagged rocks of all sizes have fallen in what must have been
a monstrous cave-in blocking the tunnel to the east. The subterranean event has
created a dead-end behind the sturdy iron portcullis to the west, though work
seems to be ongoing to clear the rubble and allow free passage once again.
name: Collapsed Cave

desc:
This tunnel climbs rapidly as it turns from east to north. Though the floor is
steep, it is comprised of solid stone and seems well worn. Piles of old, rotting
supplies sit at the east end of the tunnel near an entrance to another chamber.
The northern side of the tunnel opens onto a rope bridge over open air.
name: Steep Tunnel

desc:
A bridge of strong planks supported by sturdy ropes spans a deep ravine. The
bridge is only about ten yards long, crossing a narrow part of the gulch. The
ropes are anchored at either end to stout rods embedded into rocky ledges. The
southern ledge stands before a dark tunnel that delves into cliff walls, while
the northern end of the bridge connects to a steep mountainside.
name: Rope Bridge

desc:
Huge stone slabs appear cracked and broken by great tree trunks that spring from
cracks in the bedrock of a weathered mountainside. A rope bridge extends away
south across a deep ravine, while a steep cliff descends westward away from the
inaccessible peak to the northeast. Crumbling stones and giant gnarled tree
roots dominate the landscape beneath the darkness created by the thick, ancient
trees.
name: Steep Mountainside

desc:
A thick haze has coalesced just above ground level as if the trees' limbs and
leaves have captured a cloud. Travel is treacherous due to the reduced
visibility compounded by gnarled, thick roots and thorny vines that drape the
ground in a jumbled fashion.
name: Misty Woods

desc:
The air is thick with the heavy musk of trolls. The rock underfoot has been
scraped smooth by the frequent shuffling of immense beings. The walls bear
jagged claw-marks while even the tall ceiling shows signs of having been gashed.
A darkly-stained path leads to the north.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
The air is thick with the heavy musk of trolls. The rock underfoot has been
scraped smooth by the frequent shuffling of immense beings. The walls bear
jagged claw-marks while even the tall ceiling shows signs of having been gashed.
A darkly-stained path leads to the south.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
A deep bowl-shaped pit occupies much of the floor space in this large cavern.
Stained and slippery with dark substances, the pit emits the odour of decay and
death. Broken bones, ragged clothing, and shattered armour line the centre of
the pit. Dark passages lead in all directions where guttural growls and heavy
shuffling sounds seem to be getting closer.
name: Dark Pit

desc:
A great chasm cleaves its way through a carved passageway running east to west,
opening suddenly in the centre of the tunnel. Neither the chasm's ceiling or
floor can be seen, hidden by a heavy gloom that shrouds both the height and
depth of the great crack itself. The delvers of this passage carved walkways
into its sides, effectively creating a ring of narrow shelves. The hewn stone
ring creates a strong natural chokepoint at the eastern end where the two paths
meet that has been further fortified with a wooden defensive structure.
name: Sombre Passageway at a Chasm
